name: hatchling

metadata.name: hatchling
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# Hatchling
| | | | --- | --- | | Package | [![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/hatchling.svg?logo=pypi&label=PyPI&logoColor=gold)](https://pypi.org/project/hatchling/) [![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/hatchling.svg?color=blue&label=Downloads&logo=pypi&logoColor=gold)](https://pypi.org/project/hatchling/) [![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/hatchling.svg?logo=python&label=Python&logoColor=gold)](https://pypi.org/project/hatchling/) | | Meta | [![Hatch project](https://img.shields.io/badge/%F0%9F%A5%9A-Hatch-4051b5.svg)](https://github.com/pypa/hatch) [![linting - Ruff](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/charliermarsh/ruff/main/assets/badge/v2.json)](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff) [![code style - Black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black) [![types - Mypy](https://img.shields.io/badge/types-Mypy-blue.svg)](https://github.com/python/mypy) [![License - MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-9400d3.svg)](https://spdx.org/licenses/) [![GitHub Sponsors](https://img.shields.io/github/sponsors/ofek?logo=GitHub%20Sponsors&style=social)](https://github.com/sponsors/ofek) |
----- This is the extensible, standards compliant build backend used by [Hatch](https://github.com/pypa/hatch). ## Usage The following snippet must be present in your project's `pyproject.toml` file in order to use Hatchling as your build backend: ```toml [build-system] requires = ["hatchling"] build-backend = "hatchling.build" ``` Then a build frontend like [pip](https://github.com/pypa/pip), [build](https://github.com/pypa/build), or Hatch itself can build or install your project automatically: ```console # install using pip pip install /path/to/project # build python -m build /path/to/project # build with Hatch hatch build /path/to/project ``` ## Documentation - [Project metadata](https://hatch.pypa.io/latest/config/metadata/) - [Dependencies](https://hatch.pypa.io/latest/config/dependency/) - [Packaging](https://hatch.pypa.io/latest/config/build/)

functions.name: hatchling.builders.utils.get_reproducible_timestamp
documentation:
Returns an `int` derived from the `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH` environment variable; see
https://reproducible-builds.org/specs/source-date-epoch/.

The default value will always be: `1580601600`

functions.name: hatchling.builders.utils.normalize_file_permissions
documentation:
https://github.com/takluyver/flit/blob/6a2a8c6462e49f584941c667b70a6f48a7b3f9ab/flit_core/flit_core/common.py#L257

Normalize the permission bits in the st_mode field from stat to 644/755.

Popular VCSs only track whether a file is executable or not. The exact
permissions can vary on systems with different umasks. Normalizing
to 644 (non executable) or 755 (executable) makes builds more reproducible.

classes.name: hatchling.builders.sdist.SdistBuilder
documentation: Build an archive of the source files

classes.name: hatchling.builders.plugin.interface.BuilderInterface

methods.name: hatchling.builders.plugin.interface.BuilderInterface.recurse_included_files
documentation:
Returns a consistently generated series of file objects for every file that should be distributed. Each file
object has three `str` attributes:

- `path` - the absolute path
- `relative_path` - the path relative to the project root; will be an empty string for external files
- `distribution_path` - the path to be distributed as

methods.name: hatchling.builders.plugin.interface.BuilderInterface.root
documentation: The root of the project tree.

methods.name: hatchling.builders.plugin.interface.BuilderInterface.get_version_api
documentation:
A mapping of `str` versions to a callable that is used for building.
Each callable must have the following signature:

```python
def ...(build_dir: str, build_data: dict) -> str:
```

The return value must be the absolute path to the built artifact.

methods.name: hatchling.builders.plugin.interface.BuilderInterface.get_default_versions
documentation: A list of versions to build when users do not specify any, defaulting to all versions.

methods.name: hatchling.builders.plugin.interface.BuilderInterface.get_default_build_data
documentation: A mapping that can be modified by [build hooks](../build-hook/reference.md) to influence the behavior of builds.

methods.name: hatchling.builders.plugin.interface.BuilderInterface.clean
documentation:
Called before builds if the `-c`/`--clean` flag was passed to the
[`build`](../../cli/reference.md#hatch-build) command.

Example usage:

```python tab="plugin.py"
from hatchling.builders.plugin.interface import BuilderInterface


class SpecialBuilder(BuilderInterface):
    PLUGIN_NAME = 'special'
    ...
```

```python tab="hooks.py"
from hatchling.plugin import hookimpl

from .plugin import SpecialBuilder


@hookimpl
def hatch_register_builder():
    return SpecialBuilder
```

methods.name: hatchling.builders.config.BuilderConfig.only_packages
documentation: Whether or not the target should ignore non-artifact files that do not reside within a Python package.

methods.name: hatchling.builders.config.BuilderConfig.reproducible
documentation: Whether or not the target should be built in a reproducible manner, defaulting to true.

methods.name: hatchling.builders.config.BuilderConfig.dev_mode_dirs
documentation:
Directories which must be added to Python's search path in
[dev mode](../config/environment/overview.md#dev-mode).

classes.name: hatchling.builders.hooks.plugin.interface.BuildHookInterface

methods.name: hatchling.builders.hooks.plugin.interface.BuildHookInterface.root
documentation: The root of the project tree.

methods.name: hatchling.builders.hooks.plugin.interface.BuildHookInterface.target_name
documentation: The plugin name of the build target.

methods.name: hatchling.builders.hooks.plugin.interface.BuildHookInterface.dependencies
documentation:
A list of extra [dependencies](../../config/dependency.md) that must be installed
prior to builds.

!!! warning
    - For this to have any effect the hook dependency itself cannot be dynamic and
        must always be defined in `build-system.requires`.
    - As the hook must be imported to call this method, imports that require these
        dependencies must be evaluated lazily.

methods.name: hatchling.builders.hooks.plugin.interface.BuildHookInterface.clean
documentation:
This occurs before the build process if the `-c`/`--clean` flag was passed to
the [`build`](../../cli/reference.md#hatch-build) command, or when invoking
the [`clean`](../../cli/reference.md#hatch-clean) command.

methods.name: hatchling.builders.hooks.plugin.interface.BuildHookInterface.initialize
documentation:
This occurs immediately before each build.

Any modifications to the build data will be seen by the build target.

methods.name: hatchling.builders.hooks.plugin.interface.BuildHookInterface.finalize
documentation:
This occurs immediately after each build and will not run if the `--hooks-only` flag
was passed to the [`build`](../../cli/reference.md#hatch-build) command.

The build data will reflect any modifications done by the target during the build.

Example usage:

```python tab="plugin.py"
from hatchling.builders.hooks.plugin.interface import BuildHookInterface


class SpecialBuildHook(BuildHookInterface):
    PLUGIN_NAME = 'special'
    ...
```

```python tab="hooks.py"
from hatchling.plugin import hookimpl

from .plugin import SpecialBuildHook


@hookimpl
def hatch_register_build_hook():
    return SpecialBuildHook
```

functions.name: hatchling.plugin.specs.hatch_register_version_source
documentation: Register new classes that adhere to the version source interface.

functions.name: hatchling.plugin.specs.hatch_register_builder
documentation: Register new classes that adhere to the builder interface.

functions.name: hatchling.plugin.specs.hatch_register_build_hook
documentation: Register new classes that adhere to the build hook interface.

functions.name: hatchling.plugin.specs.hatch_register_metadata_hook
documentation: Register new classes that adhere to the metadata hook interface.

classes.name: hatchling.bridge.app.Application

methods.name: hatchling.bridge.app.Application.verbosity
documentation: The verbosity level of the application, with 0 as the default.

methods.name: hatchling.bridge.app.Application.display_info
documentation: Meant to be used for messages conveying basic information.

methods.name: hatchling.bridge.app.Application.display_waiting
documentation: Meant to be used for messages shown before potentially time consuming operations.

methods.name: hatchling.bridge.app.Application.display_success
documentation: Meant to be used for messages indicating some positive outcome.

methods.name: hatchling.bridge.app.Application.display_warning
documentation: Meant to be used for messages conveying important information.

methods.name: hatchling.bridge.app.Application.display_error
documentation: Meant to be used for messages indicating some unrecoverable error.

methods.name: hatchling.bridge.app.Application.display_debug
documentation:
Meant to be used for messages that are not useful for most user experiences.
The `level` option must be between 1 and 3 (inclusive).

methods.name: hatchling.bridge.app.Application.abort
documentation: Terminate the program with the given return code.

The way output is displayed can be [configured](../config/hatch.md#terminal) by users.

!!! important
    Never import this directly; Hatch judiciously decides if a type of plugin requires
    the capabilities herein and will grant access via an attribute.

methods.name: hatchling.utils.context.ContextFormatter.get_formatters
documentation:
This returns a mapping of supported field names to their respective formatting functions. Each function
accepts 2 arguments:

- the `value` that was passed to the format call, defaulting to `None`
- the modifier `data`, defaulting to an empty string

classes.name: hatchling.version.scheme.plugin.interface.VersionSchemeInterface

methods.name: hatchling.version.scheme.plugin.interface.VersionSchemeInterface.root
documentation: The root of the project tree as a string.

methods.name: hatchling.version.scheme.plugin.interface.VersionSchemeInterface.update
documentation:
This should return a normalized form of the desired version and verify that it
is higher than the original version.

Example usage:

```python tab="plugin.py"
from hatchling.version.scheme.plugin.interface import VersionSchemeInterface


class SpecialVersionScheme(VersionSchemeInterface):
    PLUGIN_NAME = 'special'
    ...
```

```python tab="hooks.py"
from hatchling.plugin import hookimpl

from .plugin import SpecialVersionScheme


@hookimpl
def hatch_register_version_scheme():
    return SpecialVersionScheme
```

classes.name: hatchling.version.source.plugin.interface.VersionSourceInterface

methods.name: hatchling.version.source.plugin.interface.VersionSourceInterface.root
documentation: The root of the project tree as a string.

methods.name: hatchling.version.source.plugin.interface.VersionSourceInterface.get_version_data
documentation:
This should return a mapping with a `version` key representing the current version of the project and will be
displayed when invoking the [`version`](../../cli/reference.md#hatch-version) command without any arguments.

The mapping can contain anything else and will be passed to
[set_version](reference.md#hatchling.version.source.plugin.interface.VersionSourceInterface.set_version)
when updating the version.

methods.name: hatchling.version.source.plugin.interface.VersionSourceInterface.set_version
documentation: This should update the version to the first argument with the data provided during retrieval.

Example usage:

```python tab="plugin.py"
from hatchling.version.source.plugin.interface import VersionSourceInterface


class SpecialVersionSource(VersionSourceInterface):
    PLUGIN_NAME = 'special'
    ...
```

```python tab="hooks.py"
from hatchling.plugin import hookimpl

from .plugin import SpecialVersionSource


@hookimpl
def hatch_register_version_source():
    return SpecialVersionSource
```

methods.name: hatchling.metadata.plugin.interface.MetadataHookInterface.get_known_classifiers

Example usage:

```python tab="plugin.py"

    ...
```


functions.name: hatchling.metadata.utils.is_valid_project_name
documentation: NOTE: this module should rarely be changed because it is likely to be used by other packages like Hatch
